compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Three low-severity test-quality issues from the validation are worth cleaning up:

- The post-deletion smoke test
  (`tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_post_deletion_smoke.py`) invokes each leaf
  but catches/ignores every exception except `ModuleNotFoundError` and asserts only that
  the registry has `>=6` entries — it is linkage coverage but is named/positioned as if
  it proves every leaf is invocable.
- `test_repair_json_receives_schema` only asserts the schema object is forwarded as a
  kwarg; it never shows schema guidance changes a parsed result.
- `test_capture_baseline_not_implemented_error_not_raised` asserts only `is not None`,
  duplicating stronger neighboring baseline tests.

## What

- Rename/rescope the smoke test to what it actually proves, add a behavioral
  schema-guided parse test, and remove/fold the redundant baseline test.

## Scope

- `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_post_deletion_smoke.py`
- `tests/unit/test_silent_success.py`
- `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_health_baseline.py`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The post-deletion smoke test is renamed to reflect stale-import/linkage detection
      and its docstring/coverage note scopes it to linkage only (prefer importing/
      resolving referenced modules over pretend-executing every leaf).
- [ ] A new behavioral test pins schema-guided parsing with a CONCRETE, committed
      fixture: schema
      `{"type":"object","properties":{"count":{"type":"integer"}},"required":["count"]}`
      (or the equivalent Pydantic model), malformed/ambiguous payload `'{"count": "3"}'`,
      expected validated object `count == 3` (int). The primary, version-stable assertion
      is that `parse_structured(<model>, payload)` returns the coerced model
      (`count=3:int`) while `tolerant_parse(payload)` with NO schema returns
      `{"count": "3"}` (string) — a deterministic, installed-library-independent
      difference. IF a payload is also found for which `json_repair.repair_json(schema=)`
      itself differs from the schema-less call on the installed version, assert that too;
      otherwise the AC is satisfied by the `parse_structured` coercion assertion and the
      finding is noted in the test. The forwarding spy is kept only as a narrow
      integration check.
- [ ] `test_capture_baseline_not_implemented_error_not_raised` is deleted or folded into
      the strongest baseline test.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
compiled_state.title: Test-suite hygiene: post-deletion smoke, schema-guided parse, redundant baseline
